method_atom: A selection-driven ordering transition in a noisy communication channel: a population of competing agents each carries a stochastic map from a fixed set of input symbols to a smaller set of output labels, and selection for a fitness that penalizes confusion-induced errors drives a supercritical, symmetry-breaking phase transition at which the map becomes nonrandom. Near the critical point a small-parameter expansion is valid and the emergent structure is governed by the smooth (low-lying) eigenmodes of the graph Laplacian of a confusion/error graph, whose topology sets an upper bound on the number of distinguishable output labels via a map-coloring constraint.
object_atom: The genetic code: the mapping of the sixty-four nucleotide triplets (codons) to twenty amino acids in evolving, competing organisms, with a suggested extension to other error-prone biological codes such as protein recognition of DNA sites in transcription-regulatory networks.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the error-minimizing noisy-channel map that orders at a supercritical transition governed by low error-graph-Laplacian modes and a map-coloring bound; the domain-specific object is the codon-to-amino-acid genetic code carried by evolving organisms.
